question: What is the main difference between Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and Life-Cycle Assessment (LCA) ?

options.1: EIA focuses only on product design, while LCA focuses on project location
options.2: EIA and LCA both focus only on air pollution levels
options.3: EIA evaluates environmental impacts before a project starts, while LCA assesses impacts throughout the entire life of a product or process
options.4: EIA is used for agricultural projects only, while LCA is used for industrial projects only

question: How do Green Chemistry and Cleaner Production Technologies support the goals of EIA and LCA ?

options.1: By promoting the use of hazardous chemicals for faster production
options.2: By ignoring product life-cycle impacts and focusing only on profit
options.3: By encouraging disposal of industrial waste into nearby water bodies
options.4: By reducing waste generation, minimizing pollution and ensuring sustainability throughout a product's life cycle
